title: Product: The Dynamic Amount‑Suggestion feature automatically shows ## Feature The **Dynamic Amount‑Suggestion** feature automatically shows
subtitle: Product: The Dynamic Amount‑Suggestion feature automatically shows The Dynamic Amount‑Suggestion feature automatically s

document_meta.recipient_line: All Member Banks, PSP Banks and UPI Applications
document_meta.subject_line: Subject: Product: The Dynamic Amount‑Suggestion feature automatically shows ## Feature The **Dynamic Amount‑Suggestion** feature automatically shows
document_meta.desired_outcome: Complete document suite for Smart Amount Suggestions

sections.heading: Letterhead & Reference Block
sections.content_instructions:
Prepare the issuing organization header details. Include only the minimum official identity elements needed for traceability.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE TECHNICAL SPECIFICATIONS (derived from NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Participant Obligations:
  PSP Banks: must implement the suggestion retrieval API using NPCI transaction history.; must enforce UPI PIN for amounts >15,000 as per existing rules.
  Issuer Banks: must expose historical transaction data to NPCI for authorized PSPs.; must respond to debit/credit requests in accordance with standard timelines.
  NPCI: will route suggestion requests and provide transaction history securely.; will validate amount thresholds and PIN requirements as per existing specifications.

Go-Live Timeline: Not specified — omit dates
Supersedes: None
RULE: Terse, formal directive language. Use 'must' for mandatory, 'are advised to' for recommended.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required]** We assume the payer will be identified by the standard UPI Virtual Payment Address (VPA) – the same identifier used in the existing ReqPay message. Confirm, or specify a different identifier type (e.g., mobile number, Aadhaar‑linked ID).
A: yes proceed

Q: **[Required]** We assume the payee will also be identified by a VPA, matching the current payeeVPA field in the ReqPay request. Confirm, or indicate if an alternative identifier (e.g., merchant ID, phone number) is required.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** For the smart‑suggested‑amount flow we will apply the same per‑transaction ceiling as the baseline UPI rule – INR 15,000 – with mandatory PIN enforcement above that limit. Confirm, or provide a different maximum amount that should apply specifically to suggested‑amount payments.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** We will inherit the payer’s existing daily UPI aggregate limit set by the PSP (e.g., ₹1 lakh per day) for transactions generated via the suggestion UI. Confirm, or specify a distinct daily cap that should be enforced for this feature.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** To deliver the amount suggestions we propose adding two new API messages:  
   * **SuggestionRequest** – sent from the payer PSP to NPCI (or a designated suggestion service) with payerVPA and payeeVPA to retrieve historic amounts.  
   * **SuggestionResponse** – returns a list (up to three) of suggested amounts plus optional metadata (frequency, recency).  

   The existing ReqPay flow will remain unchanged after the user selects a suggestion. Confirm, or outline any additional request/response fields or alternative message names you prefer.
A: yes
sections.prompt_instruction: Include the issuing organization name, a formal circular reference number in the format [ORG]/[DEPT]/OC No. [NNN]/[YYYY-YYYY], and the issue date flush-right. This header is mandatory and must appear before all body text.

sections.heading: Context Paragraph
sections.content_instructions:
Explain the current state, the ecosystem gap, and why the issuer is issuing this directive. Keep it factual and vendor-neutral.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Current State: Today a UPI payment follows a multi‑step flow where the payer app sends a ReqPay to its PSP, NPCI obtains authentication from the payee PSP via ReqAuthDetail, the payer PSP then sends a debit request (ReqDebit) to NPCI, and finally credit is posted to the merchant through ReqCredit. Amount entry is manual and the app validates PIN only when the amount exceeds the configured threshold.
Limitations / Pain Points: Manual amount entry leads to slower checkout and higher typo‑related failures. PSPs and merchants have no visibility into the user's historical payment patterns, missing an opportunity to streamline repeat payments. The current flow also does not provide a UI mechanism for one‑tap selection of frequently used amounts.
RULE: Terse, formal directive language. Use 'must' for mandatory obligations.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required]** We assume the payer will be identified by the standard UPI Virtual Payment Address (VPA) – the same identifier used in the existing ReqPay message. Confirm, or specify a different identifier type (e.g., mobile number, Aadhaar‑linked ID).
A: yes proceed

Q: **[Required]** We assume the payee will also be identified by a VPA, matching the current payeeVPA field in the ReqPay request. Confirm, or indicate if an alternative identifier (e.g., merchant ID, phone number) is required.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** For the smart‑suggested‑amount flow we will apply the same per‑transaction ceiling as the baseline UPI rule – INR 15,000 – with mandatory PIN enforcement above that limit. Confirm, or provide a different maximum amount that should apply specifically to suggested‑amount payments.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** We will inherit the payer’s existing daily UPI aggregate limit set by the PSP (e.g., ₹1 lakh per day) for transactions generated via the suggestion UI. Confirm, or specify a distinct daily cap that should be enforced for this feature.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** To deliver the amount suggestions we propose adding two new API messages:  
   * **SuggestionRequest** – sent from the payer PSP to NPCI (or a designated suggestion service) with payerVPA and payeeVPA to retrieve historic amounts.  
   * **SuggestionResponse** – returns a list (up to three) of suggested amounts plus optional metadata (frequency, recency).  

   The existing ReqPay flow will remain unchanged after the user selects a suggestion. Confirm, or outline any additional request/response fields or alternative message names you prefer.
A: yes
sections.prompt_instruction: Describe the current state, identify the limitation or opportunity, and briefly state what the issuer has decided to do in response. Single paragraph, 3-5 sentences.

sections.heading: Decision & Scope Statement
sections.content_instructions:
State the decision declaratively and name the technical artifacts, modules, schemas, or APIs affected.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Functional Requirements:
  FR-01: The system shall retrieve the payer's last N transaction amounts for a given payee from the NPCI transaction history.
  FR-02: The system shall display up to three suggested amounts on the payment screen, ranked by recency and frequency.
  FR-03: The payer shall be able to select a suggested amount with a single tap.
  FR-04: The selected amount shall be editable before final confirmation, preserving the one‑tap convenience.
  FR-05: The payment request generated from a suggested amount shall follow the existing ReqPay → ReqAuthDetail → ReqDebit flow unchanged.
  FR-06: For suggested amounts >15,000, the UI shall enforce mandatory UPI PIN entry as per existing PIN rules.
RULE: Terse, formal directive language. Use 'must' for mandatory obligations.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required]** We assume the payer will be identified by the standard UPI Virtual Payment Address (VPA) – the same identifier used in the existing ReqPay message. Confirm, or specify a different identifier type (e.g., mobile number, Aadhaar‑linked ID).
A: yes proceed

Q: **[Required]** We assume the payee will also be identified by a VPA, matching the current payeeVPA field in the ReqPay request. Confirm, or indicate if an alternative identifier (e.g., merchant ID, phone number) is required.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** For the smart‑suggested‑amount flow we will apply the same per‑transaction ceiling as the baseline UPI rule – INR 15,000 – with mandatory PIN enforcement above that limit. Confirm, or provide a different maximum amount that should apply specifically to suggested‑amount payments.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** We will inherit the payer’s existing daily UPI aggregate limit set by the PSP (e.g., ₹1 lakh per day) for transactions generated via the suggestion UI. Confirm, or specify a distinct daily cap that should be enforced for this feature.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** To deliver the amount suggestions we propose adding two new API messages:  
   * **SuggestionRequest** – sent from the payer PSP to NPCI (or a designated suggestion service) with payerVPA and payeeVPA to retrieve historic amounts.  
   * **SuggestionResponse** – returns a list (up to three) of suggested amounts plus optional metadata (frequency, recency).  

   The existing ReqPay flow will remain unchanged after the user selects a suggestion. Confirm, or outline any additional request/response fields or alternative message names you prefer.
A: yes
